title: Hero Diver's Incredible Underwater Rescue
story: Splash! A trolleybus plunged into a reservoir in Erevan. But on September 16, 1976, an amazing hero emerged. Shavarsh Karapetyan, a champion swimmer, dove into action. Without hesitation, he plunged into the murky depths. Again and again, Shavarsh swam down, breaking windows and pulling people to safety. His bravery and strength saved 20 lives that day. Shavarsh's selfless act reminds us that ordinary people can do extraordinary things.
activity_question: Who was the hero that saved people from the sunken trolleybus?
follow_up_question: What special skill did Shavarsh have that helped him save people?

title: Daring Balloon Escape to Freedom
story: Two families soared to freedom in a homemade hot air balloon! On September 16, 1979, the Strelzyks and Wetzels floated silently through the night sky, crossing from East Germany to West Germany. They had secretly built their escape craft using bedsheets and a makeshift burner. After a tense flight, they landed safely, leaving behind the strict rules of Communist East Germany for a new life of freedom in the West.

title: Curious George's Creator Swings into Life
story: A mischievous monkey named Curious George captured the hearts of children worldwide, but did you know his creator was born on September 16, 1898? H. A. Rey, the American children's author behind the beloved character, entered the world in Hamburg, Germany. Rey's playful imagination and artistic talent would later bring joy to countless young readers through his adventurous little primate pal.

title: Mosquito-Busting Success in Africa
story: Buzz off, malaria! A report from the University of Oxford brought amazing news in 2015. Since the year 2000, Africa had prevented a whopping 700 million cases of this mosquito-spread illness. title: Zoom! The Fastest Car on Earth
story: Vroom! The ground shook as George Eyston's car roared across the salt flats. On September 16, 1938, this British racing driver pushed the limits of speed. His specially designed Thunderbolt car zoomed to an incredible 357.5 miles per hour. That's faster than a cheetah, faster than a race horse, and even faster than some airplanes! Eyston's daring feat set a new world auto speed record, showing just how far car technology had come.

title: Phillies' Wild Comeback Victory
story: Bats were swinging and the crowd was roaring! In 1930, on September 16, the Philadelphia Phillies pulled off an incredible baseball comeback. title: The Bell That Sparked a Revolution
story: A brave priest named Miguel Hidalgo y Costilla set off a chain of events that would change Mexico forever. On September 16, 1810, he rang the church bells in Dolores, gathering people for an important announcement. His passionate speech, known as the Grito de Dolores or 'Cry of Dolores', called for an end to Spanish rule. This bold act sparked the Mexican War of Independence and is now celebrated as Mexican Independence Day.

title: BART Dives Under the Bay
story: Whoosh! In 1974, a new kind of train zipped under the San Francisco Bay for the first time. On September 16, the Bay Area Rapid Transit (BART) system opened its Transbay Tube. This underwater tunnel let trains zoom between San Francisco and Oakland in minutes. It was like a secret passage beneath the waves, carrying commuters and adventurers alike. BART's underwater journey changed how people traveled around the bay, making the region feel more connected than ever before.
activity_question: What did BART's Transbay Tube allow trains to do?
activity_choices: Zoom between San Francisco and Oakland
Float on top of the water
activity_answer: Zoom between San Francisco and Oakland
bonus_fact: The Transbay Tube sits about 135 feet below the surface of the bay at its deepest point.

title: Nature's Mighty Power: A Record-Breaking Storm
story: The air pressure plummeted to an astonishing low near Okinawa in 1945. On September 16, instruments recorded a reading of just 856 millibars. This incredibly low pressure marked the arrival of one of the most intense storms ever measured in that region. Such extreme weather events remind us of nature's awesome power.
activity_question: What did the instruments near Okinawa record?
follow_up_question: Why do you think extremely low air pressure is often associated with strong storms?

title: Nature's Fury: Typhoon Kathleen Strikes Japan
story: Powerful winds and raging waters swept through Japan as Typhoon Kathleen unleashed its fury in 1947. On September 16, the storm battered Saitama, Tokyo, and the Tone River area. Communities faced the typhoon's wrath, but people came together to help each other. The event led to improved weather forecasting and disaster preparedness in Japan.
activity_question: Which areas were affected by Typhoon Kathleen?
activity_choices: Saitama, Tokyo, and Tone River area
activity_answer: Saitama, Tokyo, and Tone River area
follow_up_question: How might better weather forecasting help during typhoons?

title: Rhode Island's Wettest Day Ever
story: Rain poured from the sky in Westerly, Rhode Island, setting a state record that still stands today. On September 16, 1932, nearly a foot of water drenched the coastal town. Streets turned into rivers, and umbrellas were no match for the deluge. This extreme weather event reminds us how powerful nature can be.
activity_question: What happened in Westerly, Rhode Island in 1932?
activity_choices: The town's first ice cream shop opened
follow_up_question: How might a town prepare for such heavy rainfall?
